{{Quote|Who needs a ''blaster'' when you've got an ''F-187 cutting tool''?|[[Clone Sergeant/Legends|Clone Sergeant]] [[Gaffa]]|<ref name="Repel All Boarders!">{{TCWComicCite|6.18|Repel All Boarders!}}</ref>}}
The '''F-187 fusioncutter''' was a handheld cutting tool produced by [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The F-187 [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusioncutter]] model was popular among engineers during the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Description==
The F-187 was a model of [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusioncutter]] manufactured by [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|Sorosuub Corporation]]. Designed for use in repairing vehicles and [[Starship/Legends|starships]], it was a handheld [[Cutting torch/Legends|cutting torch]] that produced a high-energy [[Plasma/Legends|plasma]] beam that was able to cut through metals, [[Duraplast/Legends|duraplast]], and reinforced materials. The cutting beam was variable, and could be adjusted in length from one to twenty [[Centimeter/Legends|centimeters]], and its width could be as narrow as a [[Millimeter/Legends|millimeter]] or up to six centimeters wide.<ref name="EGWT" />

Holes could be punched through materials but an extendable drill bit, and the entire tool could be attached to a jig connected to a [[Droid brain/Legends|droid brain]]. This allowed the F-187 to be used as an automated cutting tool. The [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] reserve for the tool allowed for an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] of continuous operation. Larger fuel cylinders could be attached to extend the tool's operational time.<ref name="EGWT" />

==History==
Such was the versatility and popularity of the F-187, it was used by a variety of factions during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], from [[clone engineer]]s to [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]]. The fusioncutter was often used to slice into a vehicle, allowing the wielder to gain control of it from its [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]].<ref name="BF II">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The F-187 serves as a tool in the ''[[Battlefront]]'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]] series. It has also appeared in the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' tie-in comic ''[[Repel All Boarders!]]''
